question: He said, 'I have been here for a week.'
options: He said that he had been there for a week.
He said that he has been there for a week.
He said that he was there for a week.
He said that I had been there for a week.
answer: He said that he had been there for a week.
explanation: Present Perfect Continuous (have been) changes to Past Perfect Continuous (had been), and 'here' changes to 'there'. ✅

question: She said, 'These books are mine.'
options: She said that those books were hers.
She said that these books were hers.
She said that those books were mine.
She said that those books are hers.
answer: She said that those books were hers.
explanation: 'These' changes to 'those', 'are' to 'were', and 'mine' to 'hers'. ✅

question: The boy said to his mother, 'I am going to school now.'
options: The boy told his mother that he was going to school then.
The boy told his mother that he was going to school now.
The boy told his mother that I am going to school then.
The boy told his mother that he is going to school then.
answer: The boy told his mother that he was going to school then.
explanation: 'Now' must change to 'then' in indirect speech when the reporting verb is in the past. ✅

question: I said to him, 'I can't give you money today.'
options: I told him that I couldn't give him money that day.
I told him that he couldn't give me money that day.
I told him that I couldn't give you money today.
I asked him that I couldn't give him money that day.
answer: I told him that I couldn't give him money that day.
explanation: 'Today' changes to 'that day' and 'you' (object) changes to 'him'. ✅

question: She said to her sister, 'I will visit you tomorrow.'
options: She told her sister that she would visit her the next day.
She told her sister that she will visit her tomorrow.
She told her sister that she would visit you the next day.
She said to her sister that she would visit her tomorrow.
answer: She told her sister that she would visit her the next day.
explanation: Will ➔ Would, You ➔ Her, Tomorrow ➔ The next day. ✅

question: He said, 'I finished this work two days ago.'
options: He said that he had finished that work two days before.
He said that he finished that work two days ago.
He said that he had finished this work two days before.
He told that he has finished that work two days before.
answer: He said that he had finished that work two days before.
explanation: V2 (finished) ➔ Had + V3, This ➔ That, Ago ➔ Before. ✅

question: They said to us, 'We shall meet you here next week.'
options: They told us that they would meet us there the following week.
They told us that they should meet us here next week.
They told us that we would meet them there the following week.
They said that they will meet us there next week.
answer: They told us that they would meet us there the following week.
explanation: Shall ➔ Would, Here ➔ There, Next week ➔ The following week. ✅

question: The teacher said to me, 'I will not tolerate this behavior.'
options: The teacher told me that he would not tolerate that behavior.
The teacher told me that I would not tolerate this behavior.
The teacher said that he will not tolerate that behavior.
The teacher told me that he should not tolerate that behavior.
answer: The teacher told me that he would not tolerate that behavior.
explanation: Will ➔ Would, This ➔ That. ✅

question: Anil said, 'I saw him at the station three days ago.'
options: Anil said that he had seen him at the station three days before.
Anil said that he saw him at the station three days before.
Anil told that he had seen him at the station three days ago.
Anil said that I had seen him at the station three days before.
answer: Anil said that he had seen him at the station three days before.
explanation: Saw ➔ Had seen, Ago ➔ Before. ✅

question: She said, 'My father died a year ago.'
options: She said that her father had died a year before.
She said that my father had died a year ago.
She said that her father died a year before.
She told that her father had died a year ago.
answer: She said that her father had died a year before.
explanation: Died ➔ Had died, Ago ➔ Before. ✅

question: The servant said to the master, 'I have cleaned all these rooms.'
options: The servant told the master that he had cleaned all those rooms.
The servant told the master that he has cleaned all these rooms.
The servant said that he had cleaned all those rooms.
The servant told the master that I had cleaned those rooms.
answer: The servant told the master that he had cleaned all those rooms.
explanation: Have cleaned ➔ Had cleaned, These ➔ Those. ✅

question: He said, 'I am busy just now.'
options: He said that he was busy just then.
He said that he is busy just now.
He said that he was busy just now.
He told that he is busy just then.
answer: He said that he was busy just then.
explanation: Am ➔ Was, Now ➔ Then. ✅

question: She said to me, 'You can stay here tonight.'
options: She told me that I could stay there that night.
She told me that you could stay here tonight.
She said that I can stay there that night.
She told me that I could stay here that night.
answer: She told me that I could stay there that night.
explanation: Can ➔ Could, Here ➔ There, Tonight ➔ That night. ✅

question: The girl said, 'I will do it now or never.'
options: The girl said that she would do it then or never.
The girl said that she will do it now or never.
The girl told that she would do it now or never.
The girl said that she should do it then or never.
answer: The girl said that she would do it then or never.
explanation: Will ➔ Would, Now ➔ Then. ✅

question: Ravi said, 'I shall go to Bengaluru tomorrow.'
options: Ravi said that he would go to Bengaluru the next day.
Ravi said that I should go to Bengaluru tomorrow.
Ravi told that he shall go to Bengaluru the next day.
Ravi said that he will go to Bengaluru the next day.
answer: Ravi said that he would go to Bengaluru the next day.
explanation: Shall ➔ Would, Tomorrow ➔ The next day. ✅

question: I said to her, 'I have seen this place before.'
options: I told her that I had seen that place before.
I told her that I have seen this place before.
I told her that she had seen that place before.
I said that I had seen this place before.
answer: I told her that I had seen that place before.
explanation: Have seen ➔ Had seen, This ➔ That. ✅

question: He said, 'I am glad to be here this evening.'
options: He said that he was glad to be there that evening.
He said that he is glad to be here this evening.
He said that he was glad to be here that evening.
He told that he was glad to be there this evening.
answer: He said that he was glad to be there that evening.
explanation: Am ➔ Was, Here ➔ There, This ➔ That. ✅

question: She said, 'I lost my umbrella yesterday.'
options: She said that she had lost her umbrella the day before.
She said that she lost her umbrella yesterday.
She said that she had lost her umbrella yesterday.
She told that she has lost her umbrella the previous day.
answer: She said that she had lost her umbrella the day before.
explanation: Lost ➔ Had lost, Yesterday ➔ The day before / The previous day. ✅

question: They said, 'We are playing a match today.'
options: They said that they were playing a match that day.
They said that they are playing a match today.
They said that we were playing a match that day.
They told that they played a match that day.
answer: They said that they were playing a match that day.
explanation: Are playing ➔ Were playing, Today ➔ That day. ✅

question: I said to him, 'You did not return the book yesterday.'
options: I told him that he had not returned the book the day before.
I told him that he did not return the book yesterday.
I told him that I had not returned the book the previous day.
I said that he had not returned the book yesterday.
answer: I told him that he had not returned the book the day before.
explanation: Did not return ➔ Had not returned, Yesterday ➔ The day before. ✅

question: The boy said, 'I will not go to school today as I am ill.'
options: The boy said that he would not go to school that day as he was ill.
The boy said that he will not go to school today as he is ill.
The boy told that he would not go to school that day as I was ill.
The boy said that he should not go to school that day as he was ill.
answer: The boy said that he would not go to school that day as he was ill.
explanation: Will ➔ Would, Today ➔ That day, Am ➔ Was. ✅

question: He said to her, 'I gave you a pen yesterday.'
options: He told her that he had given her a pen the day before.
He told her that he gave her a pen the day before.
He told her that he has given you a pen yesterday.
He said that he had given her a pen the previous day.
answer: He told her that he had given her a pen the day before.
explanation: Gave ➔ Had given, You ➔ Her, Yesterday ➔ The day before. ✅

question: She said to me, 'I cannot help you now.'
options: She told me that she could not help me then.
She told me that she cannot help me now.
She told me that I could not help her then.
She said that she could not help me now.
answer: She told me that she could not help me then.
explanation: Cannot ➔ Could not, You ➔ Me, Now ➔ Then. ✅

question: They said, 'We have been waiting here for two hours.'
options: They said that they had been waiting there for two hours.
They said that they have been waiting here for two hours.
They said that we had been waiting there for two hours.
They told that they were waiting there for two hours.
answer: They said that they had been waiting there for two hours.
explanation: Have been ➔ Had been, Here ➔ There. ✅

question: I said to him, 'I will meet you at this place tomorrow.'
options: I told him that I would meet him at that place the next day.
I told him that I would meet you at this place tomorrow.
I told him that he would meet me at that place the next day.
I said that I will meet him at that place the next day.
answer: I told him that I would meet him at that place the next day.
explanation: Will ➔ Would, You ➔ Him, This ➔ That, Tomorrow ➔ The next day. ✅

question: He said, 'I will stay here tonight.'
options: He said that he would stay there that night.
He said that he will stay here tonight.
He said that he would stay here that night.
He told that he is staying there that night.
answer: He said that he would stay there that night.
explanation: Will ➔ Would, Here ➔ There, Tonight ➔ That night. ✅

question: She said, 'I have seen this movie twice.'
options: She said that she had seen that movie twice.
She said that she has seen this movie twice.
She said that she had seen this movie twice.
She told that she saw that movie twice.
answer: She said that she had seen that movie twice.
explanation: Have seen ➔ Had seen, This ➔ That. ✅

question: The man said, 'I was working in this office five years ago.'
options: The man said that he had been working in that office five years before.
The man said that he was working in that office five years before.
The man said that he had been working in this office five years ago.
The man told that he has been working in that office five years before.
answer: The man said that he had been working in that office five years before.
explanation: Was working ➔ Had been working, This ➔ That, Ago ➔ Before. ✅

question: He said to his father, 'I need some money now.'
options: He told his father that he needed some money then.
He told his father that he needs some money now.
He told his father that I needed some money then.
He told his father that he needed some money now.
answer: He told his father that he needed some money then.
explanation: Need ➔ Needed, Now ➔ Then. ✅

question: They said, 'We went to Delhi last year.'
options: They said that they had gone to Delhi the previous year.
They said that they went to Delhi last year.
They said that we had gone to Delhi the previous year.
They told that they have gone to Delhi the year before.
answer: They said that they had gone to Delhi the previous year.
explanation: Went ➔ Had gone, Last year ➔ The previous year / The year before. ✅
